creator: alt2600@icloud.com
text:
(In reply to Tobias C. Berner from comment #11)


No thank you, I looked at the old Makefile and it is a nightmare most unholy. No way will I re-implement that. A reasonable middleground that doesn't require hacking in patches to third party configured downloads seems to be possible. 

I just want the port to build on my machine, As I said, all I care about is adding Atlas, as openblas is a non-starter for me, Atlas was easy to support, just needed to correct searchpaths in the cmake files. I did make a few corrections to my posted patch my next one will cover LOCALBASE instead of PREFIX. Option wise make simple knobs with the WITH_ cmake booleans into options, and turn off whatever is causing problems, or just found by cmake regardless of being enabled. I an already planning on killing things like opengl, unless it proves trivial to get GTK or QT going with uses directives. Its amazing how many optional features are set to enable by default, this things cmake scripts find and try to link with everything on your system without tracking the dependency by pkgng. And if if cannot find it decide to just build its own copy of it. I have the basic options framed out, now I am confirming they configure/build correctly and package. If someone wants all that other third party stuff let them step up to maintain. I promise I'm not trying to deliver a nightmare of a Makefile.
